purpose: Line-source pressure response inputs for Northern Lights pressure screening. The model estimates p(r,t) and bottomhole pressure for a single injecting well; the same formula is also exposed through simulator reservoir and well snapshots.

model_scope.formula: Infinite-acting radial line-source solution for a constant-rate single-phase injector.
model_scope.reservoir_pressure_definition: p(r,t) at selected observation radii, not closed-boundary average reservoir pressure.
model_scope.rate_definition: total_injection_rate_mtpa is divided by active_wells before applying the single-well line-source formula.

project.value: Northern Lights CO2 transport and storage, Aurora storage complex, EL001
project.source: Northern Lights What we do; Equinor Northern Lights facts and figures

phase1_capacity_mtpa.source: Northern Lights What we do; Equinor Northern Lights facts and figures

phase2_min_capacity_mtpa.source: Northern Lights What we do; Equinor Northern Lights facts and figures

storage_depth_m_below_seabed_equinor.source: Equinor Northern Lights facts and figures

confirmation_well.value: Eos confirmation well drilled in March 2020; Northern Lights states it confirmed reservoir characteristics and storage capacity.
confirmation_well.source: Northern Lights What we do; Northern Lights About Longship; Equinor Northern Lights facts and figures

phase1_wells.source: Northern Lights What we do; local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md

concept_report_storage_target_mt.source: Northern-Lights-Project-Concept-report.pdf, p.31; stated as selecting a storage location giving potential storage of at least 100 Mt

concept_report_well_distance_south_of_no.source: Northern-Lights-Project-Concept-report.pdf, p.37; also supported by Vebenstad et al. containment risk paper, p.2

concept_report_storage_accumulation_dept.source: Northern-Lights-Project-Concept-report.pdf, p.37; approximate updip accumulation depth below Troll reservoirs

vebenstad_predicted_plume_case.value: Predicted expected CO2 plume extension after 25 years of injection, total 37.5 Mt, shown on cross-section.
vebenstad_predicted_plume_case.source: Vebenstad et al., Containment Risk Assessment of the Northern Lights Aurora CO2 Storage Site, Fig. 1, p.3

pressure_range_bar.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, based on Northern Lights facility presentation note

temperature_c.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model table

temperature_approx_c.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, based on Northern Lights facility presentation note

formations.value: Johansen and Cook sandstones as main reservoirs; Drake Formation as main caprock.
formations.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model table

johansen_cook_thickness_m.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model table

porosity_range_fraction.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model table

horizontal_permeability_range_md.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model table

line_source_input_audit.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing reported Aurora pressure range 200-300 bar
line_source_input_audit.rationale: The pressure range is sourced, but 260 bar is a selected representative value rather than a directly reported initial pressure at the modelled well.

line_source_input_audit.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model horizontal permeability range 0.1-500 mD
line_source_input_audit.rationale: The range is model-based; the selected 100 mD is a moderate screening value rather than the optimistic upper bound or a calibrated effective well-test permeability.

line_source_input_audit.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model Johansen/Cook thickness
line_source_input_audit.rationale: Reported as a model value, but effective injection thickness near the well may differ.

line_source_input_audit.source: Local docs/northern_lights_data_summary.md, summarizing Marashi Aurora model porosity range 0.073-0.314
line_source_input_audit.rationale: The range is model-based; 0.22 is a representative selected value.

line_source_input_audit.source: Engineering assumption for brine/rock/CO2 system compressibility
line_source_input_audit.rationale: No Northern Lights-specific compressibility value was found in the reviewed sources.

line_source_input_audit.source: Engineering assumption for dense/supercritical CO2 near 260 bar and 98-100 C
line_source_input_audit.rationale: Should be recomputed from an equation of state or property package for the actual CO2 composition and pressure-temperature path.

line_source_input_audit.source: Engineering assumption for dense/supercritical CO2 near 260 bar and 98-100 C
line_source_input_audit.rationale: Used only to convert mass rate to reservoir volumetric rate; should be replaced with EOS/property-package output.

line_source_input_audit.source: Northern-Lights-Project-Concept-report.pdf, p.40-p.41; derived from 8.5 inch open hole diameter in text and well schematic
line_source_input_audit.rationale: The diameter is directly shown in the concept report; the radius is a direct unit conversion.
No well-test skin or completion skin value was found; skin can strongly affect bottomhole pressure.

line_source_input_audit.source: Northern-Lights-Project-Concept-report.pdf, p.42; single well satellite phase 1 injection
line_source_input_audit.rationale: Appropriate for the Phase 1 screening case; future expansion scenarios require an updated well count.

sensitivity_cases.description: Moderate effective permeability sensitivity for pressure-risk screening.
Lower effective permeability sensitivity; still above the minimum reported grid-scale range.
Upper reported permeability sensitivity retained only as an optimistic comparison case.

limitations: Single-phase line-source model; real CO2 storage is two-phase CO2-brine displacement.
Infinite-acting reservoir; no boundaries, faults, vertical heterogeneity, gravity segregation, capillary pressure, or relative permeability.
Uses one effective permeability and one effective thickness, so results are highly sensitive to kh and viscosity.
Reservoir pressure output is p(r,t) at selected radii, not field-average reservoir pressure.
Publicly reported Northern Lights data do not provide every line-source input; assumptions are explicitly labeled.

The reviewed Concept report and Vebenstad containment-risk paper show license-scale geometry and plume migration direction, but do not provide a drainage radius or equivalent reservoir radius for the line-source model.
